section.name: Principal Grant Ceremony (AIP-GRANT)
section.t: The AIP-GRANT ceremony provides a standardised protocol for principals to authorise AI agents. AIP-GRANT is analogous to the OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code Flow, adapted for the agent identity use case. Two independent implementations following this section MUST produce interoperable grant interactions.

section.name: Overview and Roles
section.t: The AIP-GRANT ceremony involves three actors:

dl.dd: The party that constructs the GrantRequest and submits the Registration Envelope to the Registry. The deployer generates the agent's Ed25519 keypair before initiating the grant and may be the principal themselves or a service acting on the principal's behalf.
Software that holds the principal's DID private key and executes the signing ceremony. The wallet MUST verify the deployer's signature and MUST obtain explicit human approval before signing.
The service that accepts the Registration Envelope. The Registry's role is defined in ; it is not modified by AIP-GRANT.

section.artwork: Agent Deployer Principal Wallet AIP Registry | | | |-- GrantRequest ---->| | | (capabilities, | | | purpose, nonce) | | | | | | [Display consent UI] | | [Human reviews & signs] | | | | |<-- GrantResponse ---| | | (signed | | | PrincipalToken) | | | | | |-- Registration Envelope -------------->| | (identity + manifest + | | principal_token) | | | |<-- AID --------------------------------|

section.name: GrantRequest Object
section.t: The GrantRequest is a JSON object constructed by the Agent Deployer. It MUST be signed by the deployer's Ed25519 key when transmitted over the Web Redirect or QR Code bindings. The nonce MUST be cryptographically random and MUST contain at least 128 bits of entropy.

tr.td: REQUIRED for Web Redirect; MUST use HTTPS

section.t: The deployer MUST generate the agent's AID before constructing the GrantRequest so that the resulting Principal Token correctly names the agent's AID in its sub field.

section.name: Wallet Consent Requirements
section.t: The Principal Wallet MUST implement the following requirements before signing any grant.

Nonce and expiry checks: The Principal Wallet MUST check request_expires_at against the current clock. If expired, it MUST reject with grant_request_expired and MUST NOT show the consent UI. The Principal Wallet MUST maintain a record of seen grant_request_id values for at least 30 days and MUST reject replays with grant_request_replayed.
Mandatory display elements: The consent UI MUST display ALL of the following before presenting the sign/decline choice:

ol.li: Agent name (agent_name) and type (agent_type)
AI model - provider and model_id
Purpose - the purpose field verbatim
Deployer identity - deployer_name and deployer_did
All requested capabilities in canonical human-readable strings
Delegation validity - expiry computed from delegation_valid_for_seconds

Destructive Operations: Any scope marked as destructive: true in the Registry's Scope Map MUST be highlighted in the UI with a mandatory "confirm destructive action" checkbox or equivalent additional friction.

tr.td: Read your email messages and metadata
Permanently delete your email messages - this cannot be undone
Read files from your local storage
Save and modify files on your local storage
Execute scripts and commands on your system - HIGH RISK
Delete files from your local storage
Browse the web and read website content
Download files from the web to your system
Make financial transactions up to specified limits
Send and receive messages via WhatsApp
Send and receive messages via Telegram
Create child AI agents on your behalf
Monitor and manage your existing child agents

section.name: GrantResponse Object
section.t: The GrantResponse is constructed and signed by the Principal Wallet after the principal completes the signing ceremony.
Deployer validation of GrantResponse: Upon receipt the deployer MUST:

ol.li: Verify grant_request_id matches the original request
Verify nonce matches - mismatch MUST be treated as forgery
If status is "rejected", halt - no agent is registered
Decode and verify the principal_token JWT signature
Verify the principal_token payload sub matches the agent's AID

section.name: Transport Bindings
section.t: Implementations MUST support the Web Redirect Flow.
After the principal signs, the Principal Wallet delivers the GrantResponse:
The callback_uri MUST be pre-registered by the deployer. Principal Wallets MUST NOT deliver GrantResponses to unregistered URIs.

section.name: Sub-Agent Delegation Flow
section.t: When a parent agent delegates to a child agent, the parent acts as the Principal Wallet. No human consent UI is required.

ol.li: Verify requested child capabilities are a strict subset of its own Capability Manifest (Rule D-1)
Generate a fresh Ed25519 keypair for the child agent
Construct and sign the child's Principal Token
Construct the Registration Envelope for the child
Submit the Registration Envelope to the Registry
Provision the child's private key through a secure channel

section.t: The parent MUST NOT retain the child's private key after successful provisioning. Retaining the child's private key enables the parent to forge Credential Tokens in the child's name and constitutes a violation of the principle of least privilege.

section.name: G1: Registry-Mediated Grant Flow
section.t: The G1 (Registry-Mediated) grant profile is designed for consumer deployments where the agent deployer does not operate its own Principal Wallet integration. The AIP Registry brokers the consent ceremony on the deployer's behalf.
Actors: Agent Deployer, AIP Registry, Principal (via Registry-hosted or redirected wallet consent UI).
section.artwork: Deployer Registry Principal | | | |-- POST /v1/grants -->| | | (GrantRequest + | | | callback_uri) | | |<-- 201 grant_id + ---| | | wallet_redirect | | | | | |-- [redirect principal to wallet URI] ------>| | | | | |<--- authenticates ---| | |<-- approve/decline --| | | | | [Registry signs Principal Token] | | | | |<--- POST callback ---| | | (GrantResponse) | | | | | |-- GET /v1/grants/id->| | |<--- GrantResponse ---| |

ol.li: The deployer generates the agent's Ed25519 keypair and constructs a GrantRequest. For G1, callback_uri is REQUIRED.
The deployer calls POST /v1/grants. See example response.
The deployer redirects the principal to wallet_redirect_uri. The Registry presents a consent UI.
On approval, the Registry MUST construct the Principal Token and store the GrantResponse.
The deployer receives the GrantResponse and submits the Registration Envelope to POST /v1/agents with grant_tier: "G1".

section.t: GET /v1/grants/{grant_id} authorisation: Only the deployer whose deployer_did appears in the original GrantRequest MUST be permitted to retrieve the GrantResponse.
